entries.reason: Super to develop vocabulary for computer vision terms — e.g. invariant vs equivariance, in-distribution vs out-of-distribution augmentation, and very in-depth overview for augmentation
entries.summary: Albumentations' guide covers the foundational vocabulary of image augmentation: invariance (prediction unchanged by transform) vs. equivariance (prediction changes predictably with transform), and in-distribution augmentations (realistic variations that densify training data) vs. out-of-distribution augmentations (unrealistic but label-preserving transforms that act as regularizers). It organizes transforms into families — geometric, photometric, blur/noise, occlusion, and composition — all governed by one rule: a human annotator must keep the same label after the transform.

entries.reason: Wanted a reference for replacing os.path with pathlib in my projects
entries.summary: Python's pathlib module provides an object-oriented interface for filesystem paths. It offers classes like Path, PurePath, and platform-specific variants that make path manipulation more readable and less error-prone than traditional os.path string operations.

entries.reason: Rethinking how I build web UIs — maybe I don't always need a JSON API + SPA
entries.summary: This essay distinguishes between hypermedia APIs (returning HTML for browser consumption) and data APIs (returning JSON for programmatic use). It argues that many web applications are better served by hypermedia approaches, which reduce complexity by eliminating the need for client-side state management.

entries.reason: Keep forgetting einops syntax, need a quick reference
entries.summary: Einops provides a readable and unified way to manipulate tensors across NumPy, PyTorch, and TensorFlow. Its rearrange, reduce, and repeat operations use a mini-language that makes dimension transformations explicit and self-documenting, replacing chains of reshape/transpose/squeeze calls.
